rulings.Case Title: IN RE: HAWTHORNE PLACE OWNERS ASSOCIATION
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petitioners Notice Of Motion And Motion For Hearing On Verified Petition To Reduce Required Voting Percentage To Amend Declaration Of Conditions, Covenants And Restrictions Of Hawthorne Place, A Condominium Project [Cal. Civ. Code 4275]
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for July 7, 2022 line 1. NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION FOR HEARING ON VERIFIED PETITION TO REDUCE REQUIRED VOTING PERCENTAGE TO AMEND DECLARATION OF CONDITIONS, COVENANTS AND RESTRICTIONS OF HAWTHORNE PLACE, A CONDOMINIUM PROJECT is OFF CALENDAR, re-noticed for August 19, 2022. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: RUSSELL SHAWN GERNAAT VS. OCEAN AVENUE REAL ESTATE FUND, LLC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion For A Protective Order For Additional Time To Respond To Discovery And Sequencing Document Production Before Other Written Discovery Or, In The Alternative, An Order Extending Time To Respond To Discovery
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for July 7, 2022 line 2. DEFENDANTS' MOTION FOR A PROTECTIVE ORDER FOR ADDITIONAL TIME TO RESPOND TO DISCOVERY AND SEQUENCING DOCUMENT PRODUCTION BEFORE OTHER WRITTEN DISCOVERY, OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE, AN ORDER EXTENDING TIME TO RESPOND TO DISCOVERY is GRANTED in part and DENIED in part. The Motion is granted as follows: Defendants shall have until July 22, 2022 to respond. Motion is otherwise denied. Sanctions granted in the amount of $6,075 to be paid to the Plaintiff within 30 days of notice of entry of order. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: GUADALUPE OLMEDO ET AL VS. JAMES F. BLANDING ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion To Strike Punitive Damages From Plaintiffs' First Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for July 7, 2022 line 4. DEFENDANTS' MOTION TO STRIKE PUNITIVE DAMAGES FROM PLAINTIFFS' FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT is GRANTED with leave to amend for Plaintiffs to allege facts (not conclusions) giving rise to request for punitive damages, including against corporate defendants. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). rulings.Case Title: CARITAS MANAGEMENT CORPORATION VS. DALIA VILCHEZ ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE Amended ANSWER to Amended COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for July 7, 2022 line 5. PLAINTIFFS' MOTION TO STRIKE AMENDED ANSWER TO AMENDED COMPLAINT is GRANTED. New affirmative defenses added without leave of court are stricken. The Court notes that Defendant added new defenses without leave of Court in the last round of pleadings and was on notice due to a prior motion to strike that such addition is procedurally improper. Defendant again disregarded the proper procedure. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: CARITAS MANAGEMENT CORPORATION VS. DALIA VILCHEZ ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER to Amended ANSWER to Amended COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for July 7, 2022 line 5. PLAINTIFFS' DEMURRER TO AMENDED ANSWER TO AMENDED COMPLAINT is sustained in part and overruled in part. Demurrer is overruled as to the CC 1632 defense. Rescission is sufficiently stated. Demurrer is sustained with leave to amend as to estoppel defense to allege reliance. Demurrer is sustained with leave to amend as to retaliation defenses for Defendant to allege that Defendant was not in default on payment of rent (whether rent demanded by the landlord or the rent Defendant believes was due e.g. before rent increase or per tenancy at will, etc.) =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.
